notes.text: Opening skate sequence and giant amplifier: Marty is a talented but unfocused teen, already out of sync with his small-town life.
Opening image contrast: cluttered McFly house, broken car, and George's posture quietly sell 'stuck future' before we talk about time travel.
Doc hints that the future isn't written yet; choices (not fate) define who you become.
Wants music and independence but secretly fears he'll repeat his parents' failures.
Set-up at home and school: George is bullied by Biff; Lorraine is bitter; Marty's band is rejected.
Marty's fear that 'no McFly ever amounted to anything' becomes the emotional engine under every decision.
At Twin Pines Mall, the DeLorean reveal escalates quickly when Libyans arrive and Marty flees in the time machine.
Eccentric scientist whose belief in possibility contrasts with Marty's self-doubt.
Marty lands in 1955, disrupts George and Lorraine's meet-cute, and realizes he has accidentally erased his own future.
The past is fragile: small choices ripple into massive consequences.
Marty commits to fixing both the timeline and his ride home by convincing 1955 Doc and repairing the clock tower plan.
Young Doc is skeptical but inspired by Marty's proof; their partnership anchors the second act.
B-story: Marty must coach George into standing up to Biff and genuinely winning Lorraine's heart.
Plan: fake an assault so George can 'rescue' Lorraine at the dance and rewrite his own story.
Fun and Games: fish-out-of-water comedy at school, 'Calvin Klein' romance, and Biff's escalating intimidation.
Lorraine's crush on Marty complicates the plan and heightens the paradox tension.
Midpoint: under the sea dance. Marty nearly fades out of existence as the photo disappears, until George finally punches Biff and kisses Lorraine.
Act of courage rewrites destiny: George's single choice changes the entire McFly line.
Bad Guys Close In: storm, broken cable, and Biff's crew closing in create overlapping threats around the clock tower and the dance.
Ticking clock visual: repeated cuts to the clock face and lightning clouds keep the Save the Cat 'deadline' front and center.
All Is Lost: Marty believes Doc will be shot in 1985 no matter what he does; the letter is torn up in his face.
Apparent proof that the future might be fixed and sacrifice is unavoidable.
Dark Night: Marty records his goodbye on camera and prepares to risk everything on the clock tower plan anyway.
Chooses action over fear, even if it means returning to a future where Doc dies.
Break into Three: lightning strike, repaired cable, and the DeLorean hit 88 mph at the last second—Marty returns to 1985.
Callback: Twin Pines becomes Lone Pine Mall—visual proof that timelines and details really change.
Finale: Doc survives thanks to the saved letter; the improved McFly family and George's success reveal a better present.
You can rewrite the story you inherit—courage and care change family legacy.
Final Image: Marty has the truck, the girlfriend, and a revitalized family—but now knows his choices matter.
Doc returns from the future in a re-upgraded DeLorean, teasing more adventures while closing this arc cleanly.
